Title: Biosynthesis of iridoid sex pheromones in aphids
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: sex pheromone in sexual female aphids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: hind legs of sexual female aphids
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: iridoid sex pheromone biosynthesis via the mevalonate/monoterpene pathway
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: specifically expressed in hind legs of sexual female aphids
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: specifically expressed in hind legs of sexual female aphids
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: GPP (major with Co2+ or Mn2+); also FPP and GGPP, especially with Mg2+
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: condenses IPP and DMAPP to form geranyl diphosphate, with metal ion-dependent product specificity
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms the initial monoterpene precursor pool for target product biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: supplies GPP precursor for aphid iridoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: hind-leg transcriptomics; phylogenetic analysis; heterologous expression in E. coli; in vitro enzyme assay with IPP and DMAPP; LC-MS/MS product profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ApIDS was specifically expressed in hind legs of sexual female aphids. 2. Recombinant ApIDS converted IPP and DMAPP into prenyl diphosphates and favored GPP formation with cobalt or manganese. 3. ApIDS likely provides the GPP precursor for iridoid sex pheromone biosynthesis in vivo.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: sexual female-inducing short-day and low-temperature conditions used to generate the iridoid-producing morph
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ApIDS is homologous to beetle PcIDS and shows metal ion cofactor-dependent product specificity similar to PcIDS.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: sex pheromone in sexual female aphids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: hind legs of sexual female aphids
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: specifically expressed in hind legs of sexual female aphids
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: specifically expressed in hind legs of sexual female aphids
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms the monoterpene alcohol precursor for downstream iridoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: enables geraniol production for the aphid iridoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: hind-leg transcriptomics; heterologous expression in S. cerevisiae microsomes; in vitro GPP hydrolysis assay; GC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Among candidate phosphatases, only the dolichyldiphosphatase homolog hydrolyzed GPP. 2. Microsomes expressing this protein converted GPP to geraniol. 3. The enzyme was designated ApGES and substitutes for plant terpene synthases in aphids.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: sexual female-inducing short-day and low-temperature conditions
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No TPS- or HAD-like protein specifically expressed in sexual female hind legs was found; ApGES represents a noncanonical route to geraniol formation.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: sex pheromone in sexual female aphids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: hind legs of sexual female aphids
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: selectively expressed in hind legs of sexual female aphids
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: selectively expressed in hind legs of sexual female aphids
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: hydroxylates geraniol to 8-hydroxygeraniol in an NADPH-dependent reaction requiring a P450 reductase
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms a direct pathway intermediate toward 8-oxogeranial and iridoid scaffold formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: hind-leg transcriptomics; phylogenetic analysis; heterologous expression in yeast with and without ApRed; enzyme assay; GC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ApG8H was the only hind-leg-enriched P450 candidate. 2. ApG8H converted geraniol to 8-hydroxygeraniol only when coexpressed with ApRed and supplied with NADPH. 3. ApG8H also showed broader substrate acceptance for several monoterpene alcohols/alcohol-derived substrates.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: sexual female-inducing short-day and low-temperature conditions
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ApG8H shares only 35% amino acid identity with beetle PcG8H despite similar function.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: sex pheromone in sexual female aphids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: hind legs of sexual female aphids
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: selectively expressed in hind legs of sexual female aphids
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: selectively expressed in hind legs of sexual female aphids
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: catalyzes two-step NADP+-dependent oxidation of 8-hydroxygeraniol to 8-oxogeranial
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms the immediate precursor for iridoid synthase
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: produces 8-oxogeranial precursor for cyclization into the iridoid scaffold
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: hind-leg transcriptomics; heterologous expression in E. coli; purified enzyme assay with 8-hydroxygeraniol and NADP+; GC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Of several oxidase/reductase candidates, only the putative farnesol dehydrogenase oxidized 8-hydroxygeraniol. 2. ApHGO catalyzed two-step oxidation to 8-oxogeranial and preferred NADP+ over NAD+. 3. ApHGO also oxidized geraniol and nerol, indicating broader substrate range.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: sexual female-inducing short-day and low-temperature conditions
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as a soluble cytosolic protein in the proposed pathway organization.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: sex pheromone in sexual female aphids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: hind legs of sexual female aphids
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: SRD5A family-related polyprenol reductase-like membrane oxidoreductase
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: integral membrane protein with seven predicted transmembrane domains, likely ER-associated
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: selectively expressed in hind legs of sexual female aphids
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: selectively expressed in hind legs of sexual female aphids
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: cis-trans-nepetalactol (major); minor cis-trans-iridodial and unidentified compounds
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: NADPH-dependent reduction of 8-oxogeranial leading predominantly to cis-trans-nepetalactol formation in a membrane environment
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms the iridoid scaffold and immediate product nepetalactol
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: first committed step in the iridoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: catalyzes formation of the iridoid scaffold and major production of cis-trans-nepetalactol
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: hind-leg transcriptomics; heterologous expression in yeast microsomes; in vitro assay with 8-oxogeranial and NADPH; GC-MS; comparative buffer/microsome assays with CrISY; phylogenetic analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ApISY was identified as a membrane-bound polyprenol reductase-like protein specifically expressed in sexual female hind legs. 2. Yeast microsomes expressing ApISY converted 8-oxogeranial to cis-trans-nepetalactol as the major product in an NADPH-dependent reaction. 3. Membrane/lipid environment strongly favored nepetalactol formation, indicating that membrane context is functionally important for aphid iridoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: buffer concentration and lipid/microsomal environment strongly affect observed product profile; sexual female-inducing short-day and low-temperature conditions
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Likely evolved by duplication and neofunctionalization of a polyprenol reductase gene; authors cannot fully exclude contribution of other proteins to cyclization in vivo.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: sex pheromone in sexual female aphids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: hind legs of sexual female aphids
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: predicted lumen of the endoplasmic reticulum; signal peptide-containing
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: selectively expressed in hind legs of sexual female aphids
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: selectively expressed in hind legs of sexual female aphids
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms the terminal sex pheromone product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: converts nepetalactol into the terminal iridoid sex pheromone nepetalactone
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: hind-leg transcriptomics; heterologous expression in E. coli; purified enzyme assay with cis-trans-nepetalactol; GC-MS; phylogenetic analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Of the tested late-step candidates, GMC oxidase 100169582 specifically oxidized cis-trans-nepetalactol to cis-trans-nepetalactone. 2. This enzyme was designated ApNEPO. 3. ApNEPO is unrelated to plant NEPS oxidases and is predicted to reside in the ER lumen.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: sexual female-inducing short-day and low-temperature conditions
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Represents an independently evolved terminal oxidation step relative to plant pathways.
